note: Same-GPU (A6000) comparison so the QAT speed claim is fair — the main results.csv tensorrt fp16/int8 rows are from the L4 ladder. Same 1,565-image test set. Latency = warmup + sync-bracketed (harness.py). All three serve the SAME trained DINOv2-B; F1 differs only by quantization/QAT.
findings: QAT int8 is Pareto-dominant on A6000: fastest (1.66ms batch-1 / 2022 img/s batched) AND most accurate (0.8996).
PTQ int8 gets NO speed win over fp16 (2.03 vs 1.88ms batch-1; 1744 vs 1712 img/s) — TRT keeps the outlier-heavy layers in fp16, so few matmuls actually run int8.
QAT's explicit Q/DQ nodes let TRT commit every matmul to int8 tensor cores -> ~15-18% over fp16 batched (2022 vs 1712 img/s) and over PTQ int8 (2022 vs 1744).

Accuracy 0.8996 > fp16/PTQ 0.8887: QAT recovers the modelopt-PTQ collapse (0.610) AND the fine-tuning nudges the model above the fp checkpoint (0.8853). The rigorous claim: int8 no longer costs accuracy.
